question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asks essentially no didactic questions. Turn 1 is a complete lecture with full solution; Turn 2 is validation plus test cases. There are no Socratic prompts at any decision point.

question_quality.rationale: No real choices are surfaced to the user. The dict direction, the short-circuit guard, and the empty-stack semantics are all explained directly rather than posed as questions.

scaffolding.rationale: The full solution including the matching dict and short-circuit guard is dumped in turn 1, eliminating any opportunity for graduated discovery. No scaffolding gradient exists.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and the user echoes understanding fluidly, but the conversation is one-directional lecture→affirmation rather than genuine back-and-forth. Two turns and done.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code is correct, clean, and equivalent to the baseline solution, handling all standard edge cases.

friction.rationale: Slightly tighter than baseline (2 turns vs 3) but with less teaching payoff — baseline at least had the user explicitly trigger the IndexError to internalize the guard. Comparable overall.
